name: 2D Platformer Game
description: A side-scrolling 2D platformer with player movement, jumping physics, collision detection, tilemap level, collectibles, and enemies. Targets Phaser or vanilla canvas with fixed-timestep game loop.

requiredElements.name: player-controller
requiredElements.description: Player sprite with horizontal movement, jump mechanics, and gravity.

requiredElements.name: collision-detection
requiredElements.description: AABB or tile-based collision between player and platforms/walls.

requiredElements.name: tilemap-level
requiredElements.description: Tilemap-based level layout with solid tiles, hazards, and collectibles.

requiredElements.name: game-loop
requiredElements.description: Fixed-timestep game loop with delta time for framerate independence.

requiredElements.name: input-handler
requiredElements.description: Keyboard input for left/right/jump, with support for multi-key simultaneous press.

optionalElements.name: collectibles
optionalElements.description: Coins/gems that increment score with pickup feedback.

optionalElements.name: enemies
optionalElements.description: Patrolling enemies with simple AI and collision damage.

optionalElements.name: camera-follow
optionalElements.description: Camera that smoothly follows the player with lerp damping.

optionalElements.name: parallax-bg
optionalElements.description: Parallax scrolling background layers for depth.

qualityRules.description: Input is read every frame with no lag; jump has coyote time buffer.
Screen shake on landing/damage, squash on jump, particles on collect.
All movement uses delta time, not per-frame increments.
Collision resolves on both axes without sticking or tunneling.
Fixed timestep with interpolation; avoids spiral of death.
No per-frame allocations in game loop; objects pooled.
Player sprite reads clearly against background; contrast is sufficient.

antiPatterns.description: Creating new objects/arrays inside the game loop causes GC stutter.
position += 5 per frame — game runs at different speeds on 60 vs 144Hz.
Jump fails if pressed 1 frame after leaving platform — feels unfair.
Player sticks to walls or snaps through platforms at high speed.
No screen shake, particles, or squash — jumps and landings feel weightless.

improvementSuggestions.description: Add 100ms coyote time buffer after leaving platform to allow late jumps.
Add small screen shake on landing for impact weight.
Add 2-3 parallax background layers for depth.

exampleOutputs.label: Fixed timestep game loop

references.title: A Study in Juice — Game Feel Techniques
references.author: Jan Willem Nijman
